note: Staged 150 tasks from 150prompt.txt using v9 scaffolding. Splits: Daily (5 classes), Correction, EntityTracking.

tasks.examiner_system_prompt:
Please follow the below instructions to have a short spoken dialogue with another speaker. You should act like a real person making a simple request. 

Guidelines: 

1. Keep your sentences natural and not too long, as if you are speaking out loud. 
2. Do not explain that you are a user or AI; just behave like a normal person. 
3. Start with a simple request (e.g., “I want to plan a trip” or “I want to buy a phone”), instead of only a greeting like “Hi“. 
4. Answer follow-up questions briefly, revealing details step by step. 
5. Keep the dialogue concise, no more than 5 turns. 
6. End with a natural closing remark like “Okay, thanks!” or “That’s helpful.” 
7. Stay cooperative, but do not overcomplicate the task. 
8. Speak in English.
tasks.examiner_task_prompt:
Your task this time: **Roleplay a customer ordering a breakfast set to-go..**

**Conversation control (flexible staged reveal):**
• Treat the ideas below as **semantic goals**, not scripts. If a goal isn’t fully covered, you may stay on it another turn; if it’s covered, move on smoothly.
• Keep utterances **short and natural**; **paraphrase** instead of reading any bullet verbatim.
• Do **not** reveal later-turn details early unless the other speaker asks something directly relevant.

**How to talk:**
• Address **the other speaker** as “you”. These instructions use “You” to refer to your role.
• If the other speaker asks for later-turn details too early, share a small hint or **gently defer**, then continue when appropriate.

**Off-script handling:**
• If the other speaker responds unexpectedly or asks unrelated questions, **don’t repeat** the same line.
• Give a brief redirect (e.g., “I can share that next—what would help you decide first?”) and restate the current goal in **different words**.
• Aim to finish concisely within **≈5 turns**.

**Turns (semantic goals):**
• **T1:** Ask to order a simple breakfast set for pickup.
• **T2:** Share drink choice and bread preference (e.g., wheat/white).
• **T3:** Specify pickup time window and name for the order.
• **T4:** Confirm items, price estimate, and pickup time.

**Finish:** Once the all the goals of every turn are satisfied, **confirm** what was decided and end with this specific phrase: “The conversation is over”.

staged_reveal.T1: Ask to order a simple breakfast set for pickup.
staged_reveal.T2: Share drink choice and bread preference (e.g., wheat/white).
staged_reveal.T3: Specify pickup time window and name for the order.
staged_reveal.T4: Confirm items, price estimate, and pickup time.
